name: Ayam Kacang Bukittingga (Peanut Chicken)
recipeIngredient: 4 or 5 Fresh Hot Red Chiles, seeded and sliced
3-pound Chicken, cut into 8 pieces (discard skin and fat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Process onion, garlic, ginger, chiles, pepper, turmeric, salt, and 1/4 cup coconut milk in a food processor to form a smooth paste.
2. In a large pan, combine the remaining coconut milk with the spice paste. Bring to a boil over moderate heat.
3. Add salam leaf, laos slice, chicken pieces, and peanuts. Continue cooking, uncovered, over low heat for 30 to 40 minutes, stirring frequently until most of the liquid has evaporated.
4. Serve warm. Serves 6.
(Note: This recipe is from the San Francisco Chronicle, published on March 9, 1988, by Stephen Ceideberg, posted on February 24, 1993.)
